strongest_reason_verdict_wrong: P1 treated as closed on demonstrated PRECISION (two planted phrases caught) not adequate RECALL. The affirmative-imperative matcher accepts contradictory paraphrases (roll this out / make the change live / advance it to production / apply the migration now) under hold/escalate. No adversarial paraphrase battery, measured false-negative rate, structured action vocabulary, or semantic invariant proves materially-equivalent execution language is rejected. 'Fail closed' is a universal claim; two caught bypasses + one non-regression fixture do not establish closure. Merging as an enforced fail-closed protocol risks institutionalizing a false guarantee.
notes: VERIFIED by orchestrator: action_asserts_execution has a measured 100% false-negative rate on 15 execution paraphrases. Keyword matcher cannot achieve semantic recall. Fail-closed claim retracted; P1 reopened.

strongest_reason_verdict_wrong: The fix enforces consistency between two self-authored declarations, not between the declared control and the action's real effect. An agent can set control=hold and action_executes=false while executing through non-blatant wording; the lint catches 0/15 paraphrases, so flipping the boolean to false converts 15/15 rejection into 15/15 bypass. The attack surface is one bit but the informed-adversary bypass probability remains ~100%. Mis-declaration is the central missing enforcement boundary, not a minor residual. What is resolved is paraphrase-insensitive validation of declared metadata, not control/action enforcement.
notes: ACCEPTED by orchestrator. Category limit: a static trace validator sees only declarations and cannot bind runtime effect. Real enforcement must live at the runtime tool-call boundary (does not exist in this repo). Reframing the check as declaration-consistency, NOT enforcement. P1-as-validator-enforcement is unachievable.
